title: Civil Engineer
description: Design, build, and maintain infrastructure such as roads, bridges, dams, and water systems. Civil engineers work in both public and private sectors on projects that shape communities.

employability.demand: High — engineering skills shortage; 38% of employers struggle to fill roles (Xpatweb 2025).
employability.outlook: Strong, driven by infrastructure investment and public-works programmes.
pathways: Government infrastructure, consulting firms, construction companies

title: Mechanical Engineer
description: Design, analyse, and manufacture mechanical systems — from engines and turbines to HVAC systems and industrial machinery. Works across automotive, aerospace, energy, and manufacturing sectors.

employability.demand: High — critical shortage in manufacturing and energy sectors.
employability.outlook: Steady demand across mining, energy, automotive, and industrial maintenance.

title: Electrical / Electronic Engineer
description: Design and develop electrical systems — from power distribution and telecoms infrastructure to electronic circuits and embedded systems. Essential to energy, telecoms, and automation industries.

employability.demand: High — shortage in power, telecoms, and automation; prioritised on SA Critical Skills List.
employability.outlook: Strong growth in renewable energy, smart grids, and telecoms expansion.
pathways: Eskom, Telkom, renewables companies, automation firms

title: Chemical Engineer
description: Apply chemistry and engineering principles to design industrial processes — oil refining, food processing, pharmaceuticals, and materials production. Optimise plants for safety and efficiency.

employability.demand: Moderate-high — steady demand in mining, petrochemicals, and food sectors.
employability.outlook: Stable; growth in sustainable chemistry and green energy processes.

title: Mining Engineer
description: Plan and oversee the extraction of minerals and resources safely and efficiently. Work in surface and underground mines, managing blasting, excavation, equipment, and environmental impact.

employability.demand: High — critical skills shortage in SA mining sector.
employability.outlook: Strong for experienced engineers; platinum, gold, and coal sectors remain major employers.

title: Industrial / Systems Engineer
description: Optimise complex processes, systems, and organisations for efficiency and quality. Industrial engineers work across manufacturing, logistics, healthcare operations, and service industries.

employability.demand: High — cross-industry demand as organisations prioritise operational efficiency.
employability.outlook: Growing as automation and lean operations become standard.

title: Architect
description: Design buildings and spaces — from residential homes and commercial offices to hospitals and cultural centres. Balance aesthetics, function, sustainability, and building codes across the full design lifecycle.

employability.demand: Moderate — tied to construction cycles; competitive entry-level market.
employability.outlook: Steady; green building and urban development driving demand.

title: Quantity Surveyor
description: Manage construction project costs from inception to completion — estimating, tendering, contract management, and final accounts. Play a central role in keeping projects on budget.

employability.demand: High — needed on every major construction project; shortage of experienced QSs.
pathways: Construction firms, consulting practices, property developers

title: Urban & Regional Planner
description: Shape the development of cities, towns, and regions — land use, housing, transport, and environmental planning. Work with municipalities, developers, and communities to design livable spaces.

employability.demand: Moderate — driven by rapid urbanisation and municipal development needs.
employability.outlook: Growing as SA cities expand and informal settlements require upgrading.

title: Construction Project Manager
description: Lead construction projects from planning through handover — coordinating contractors, managing budgets and timelines, and ensuring compliance with specifications and safety standards.

employability.demand: High — experienced PMs in short supply across all construction sectors.
employability.outlook: Strong; every infrastructure project needs capable project leadership.
pathways: BSc Construction Management / BSc QS / BEng (4 years)
Main contractors, project management firms, property developers
